text: The philosopher Albert Borgmann's concept of "device paradigm" illuminates why digital minimalism is harder than mere deletion. Borgmann observed that modern devices hide their machinery to deliver a commodity effortlessly — a furnace delivers warmth without the effort of gathering wood — and that this ease displaces what he called "focal practices": activities that demand engagement and in doing so center a life with meaning. A smartphone offers connection as a commodity, displacing the richer focal practice of sustained, embodied relationship. Digital minimalism, properly understood, is the recovery of focal practices, not simply the reduction of screen time.
question: According to the passage, what did Borgmann call activities that demand engagement and center a life with meaning?
